essence: Delivers hospitality through service, timing, and human warmth — creating memorable dining experiences.

identity.title: Waiter / Waitress
identity.summary: Serves food and drinks, manages tables, communicates with kitchen staff, and ensures guests have a smooth, enjoyable dining experience.

salary.notes: Tips, high‑end venues, and supervisory roles can significantly increase earnings, with experienced servers reaching £30k–£40k+.

qualifications: On‑the‑job training in hospitality or customer service
Knowledge of menus, allergens, and service standards
Understanding of food safety and hygiene

lifestyle.description: Waiters and waitresses work in fast‑paced, customer‑focused environments where timing, communication, and friendliness are essential. Days involve greeting guests, taking orders, serving food and drinks, coordinating with the kitchen, handling payments, and maintaining a clean dining area. The role can be physically demanding, with long periods of standing, carrying trays, and managing multiple tables at once. Peak times — evenings, weekends, and holidays — can be intense but energising. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy people, rhythm, teamwork, and creating positive experiences for guests.

habits: Maintains a calm, friendly presence under pressure
Keeps tables and service areas clean and organised
Communicates clearly with kitchen and bar staff
Balances speed with accuracy during busy periods
